sections.paragraph:
ἀσώτως (s. ἀσωτία; Theopomp. [IV b.c.]: 115 Fgm. 224; Demosth. 40, 58) adv. of ἄσωτος ‘profligate’ (Soph. et al.; Pr 7:11; Test12Patr; Just., A I, 61, 11 of madness that knows no bounds; Tat. 12:3; loanw. in rabb.) wastefully, prodigally ζῆν of a spendthrift lifestyle (Aesop, Fab. 304 H.=169 P.; Lucian, Catapl. 17; ζῆν ἀσώτως, opp. κοσμίως Theopomp. [IV b.c.]: 115 Fgm. 224 p. 582, 34 Jac. [in Athen. 4, 62, 167c]; cp. Diogenes of Babylon, in Stoic. III 221 [in Athen. 4, 62, 168e πάντα γὰρ ἀνήλωσε τὰ πατρῷα εἰς ἀσωτίαν; in the same context 168f Athen. cites as an example of ἀσωτία provision for the washing of feet with spiced wine]; Jos., Ant. 12, 203) of a wasteful son Lk 15:13 (PFlor 99, 6ff ἐπεὶ ὁ υἱὸς ἡμῶν Κάστωρ μεθʼ ἑτέρων [Zahn ἑταιρῶν, cp. Lk 15:30] ἀσωτευόμενος ἐσπάνισε τὰ αὑτοῦ πάντα καὶ ἐπὶ τὰ ἡμῶν μεταβὰς βούλεται ἀπολέσαι κτλ.; Philo, De Prov., in Eus., PE 8, 14, 4 τῶν ἀσώτων υἱέων οὐ περιορῶσιν οἱ τοκέες); GHb 297, 20.—DELG s.v. σῶς. Spicq.

Theopomp Theopomp , hist., IV b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

Aesop Aesop = a calque for a variety of lit. associated with the name of Aesop—List 5

  1. H. = FHort, s. W-H.—List 1, beg.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Diogenes Diogenes = letters attributed to the philosopher D. of Sinope; date uncertain—List 5

Stoic Stoic , various dates—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

GHb GHb = Gospel according to the Hebrews—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 148
